Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0013",
  "title": "SECTION 3: Community",
  "page_index": 21,
  "page_range": "21-24"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0014",
    "title": "Importance of Community in Gaming",
    "page_range": "21-22",
    "summary": "This section supports retrieval for the following categories and answerable questions:

**Topics**
- Community building as a competitive strategy in gaming
- Social media vs. in-game community engagement
- Live service and event-driven retention models
- eSports as a discoverability and engagement driver
- Community management software ecosystems

**Entities**
- **Companies/Publishers:** Amber Studio, Scopely, Krafton, Epic Games, Riot Games, Roblox, Bungie, Mojang, Blizzard, Ubisoft, CD Projekt Red, Rockstar Games, Activision, Bethesda, FromSoftware, Capcom, EA, Square Enix, Garena, Hi-Rez Studios, PUBG Corp, Bandai Namco, Respawn Entertainment, Valve, Innersloth, Chucklefish, Yacht Club Games, Devolver Digital, Team Cherry, Paradox Interactive, Larian Studios, Obsidian Entertainment, Gearbox
- **Games/Titles:** Monopoly Go!, PUBG Mobile
- **Platforms/Tools:** Facebook, YouTube, TikTok, Discord, Twitch, Steam Community, Guilded, Reddit, Game Jolt, Discourse, Mobalytics
- **eSports Organizations:** TSM, Cloud9, G2 Esports, Fnatic

**Dates & Timeframes**
- July 2024: Launch of PUBG Mobile x Dragon Ball Super Z event
- Q1 2024: Monopoly Go! IAP revenue reporting period
- H1 2024: PUBG Mobile user growth and Krafton revenue reporting period
- 15 months: Timeframe for Monopoly Go! to reach $3B revenue
- 2030: Projected valuation year for the global eSports market

**Definitions & Concepts**
- Community touchpoints, live service strategy, Partner Events, cost per installs (CPI), in-app purchases (IAP), daily active users (DAUs), battle pass, IP crossover modes, eSports discoverability

**Procedures & Strategies**
- Transitioning from transactional sales to immersive community curation
- Leveraging social media for player recommendations and discussion
- Implementing in-game..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0015",
    "title": "Community Safety",
    "page_range": "23-24",
    "summary": "**Community Safety (Pages 23–24)**
**Scope & Context:** Applies globally to all Nintendo platform games; published in a 2025 industry report. Contains navigational prompts ("Book Online," "Visit Site").
**Topics:** Historical evolution of video game community safety, transition from manual to AI-driven moderation, impact of social/mobile gaming on player toxicity, and regulatory/platform safety frameworks.
**Entities:** Game genres (MMORPGs, FPS); moderation models (BPO providers, Player Experience Teams); technologies (AI, ML, NLP, image recognition, speech-to-text); regulatory bodies (EU, Ofcom, U.S. Federal Government, ESRB, PEGI, ESA); platforms (Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo).
**Dates:** 1990s (shift to online gaming), late 1990s (introduction of dedicated community managers), 2000s (rise of social/mobile gaming and BPO outsourcing), 2025 (report publication year).
**Definitions:** Community safety; Player Experience Teams (trust and safety gatekeepers); automated moderation; multimodal input detection; Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) in gaming contexts.
**Procedures:** Stepwise moderation evolution (player-led → admin-monitored → dedicated managers → in-house teams → outsourced BPO → AI/ML automation → real-time multimodal systems); Player Experience Team workflows (complaint handling, guideline enforcement, AI-driven pattern recognition, proactive flagging, prosocial behavior incentivization).
**Claims:** Increased game scale and social integration correlate with higher toxicity, cheating, and fraud; AI/ML is essential for proactive, real-time moderation across in-game and off-platform communities; regulatory compliance and platform-specific standards are critical for harm mitigation.
**Tables/Figures/Metrics/Evidence:** Tables are referenced but content is tru..."
  }
]

message.content:
**SECTION 3: Community (Pages 21–24)**
This section addresses the strategic implementation of community building in gaming and the historical-to-technological evolution of community safety frameworks. It covers engagement models, moderation workflows, regulatory compliance, and player experience management across global platforms and genres.

**Topics**
- Community building as a competitive strategy
- Social media vs. in-game community engagement
- Live service and event-driven retention models
- eSports as a discoverability and engagement driver
- Community management software ecosystems
- Historical evolution of video game community safety
- Transition from manual to AI-driven moderation
- Impact of social/mobile gaming on player toxicity
- Regulatory and platform safety frameworks

**Entities**
- **Publishers/Developers:** Amber Studio, Scopely, Krafton, Epic Games, Riot Games, Roblox, Bungie, Mojang, Blizzard, Ubisoft, CD Projekt Red, Rockstar Games, Activision, Bethesda, FromSoftware, Capcom, EA, Square Enix, Garena, Hi-Rez Studios, PUBG Corp, Bandai Namco, Respawn Entertainment, Valve, Innersloth, Chucklefish, Yacht Club Games, Devolver Digital, Team Cherry, Paradox Interactive, Larian Studios, Obsidian Entertainment, Gearbox
- **Games/Titles:** Monopoly Go!, PUBG Mobile
- **Platforms/Tools:** Facebook, YouTube, TikTok, Discord, Twitch, Steam Community, Guilded, Reddit, Game Jolt, Discourse, Mobalytics, Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo
- **eSports Organizations:** TSM, Cloud9, G2 Esports, Fnatic
- **Regulatory/Industry Bodies:** EU, Ofcom, U.S. Federal Government, ESRB, PEGI, ESA
- **Moderation/Tech Models:** BPO providers, Player Experience Teams, AI/ML/NLP systems, image recognition, speech-to-text

**Dates & Timeframes**
- 1990s: Shift to online gaming
- Late 1990s: Introduction of dedicated community managers
- 2000s: Rise of social/mobile gaming and BPO outsourcing
- Q1 2024: Monopoly Go! IAP revenue reporting period
- H1 2024: PUBG Mobile user growth and Krafton revenue reporting period
- July 2024: Launch of PUBG Mobile x Dragon Ball Super Z event
- 15 months: Timeframe for Monopoly Go! to reach $3B revenue
- 2025: Industry report publication year
- 2030: Projected valuation year for the global eSports market

**Definitions & Concepts**
- Community touchpoints, live service strategy, Partner Events, cost per installs (CPI), in-app purchases (IAP), daily active users (DAUs), battle pass, IP crossover modes, eSports discoverability, community safety, Player Experience Teams (trust and safety gatekeepers), automated moderation, multimodal input detection, Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) in gaming contexts

**Procedures & Strategies**
- Transitioning from transactional sales to immersive community curation
- Leveraging social media for player recommendations and discussion
- Implementing in-game community features and engagement loops
- Stepwise moderation evolution: player-led → admin-monitored → dedicated managers → in-house teams → outsourced BPO → AI/ML automation → real-time multimodal systems
- Player Experience Team workflows: complaint handling, guideline enforcement, AI-driven pattern recognition, proactive flagging, prosocial behavior incentivization

**Claims**
- Increased game scale and social integration correlate with higher toxicity, cheating, and fraud
- AI/ML is essential for proactive, real-time moderation across in-game and off-platform communities
- Regulatory compliance and platform-specific standards are critical for harm mitigation

**Metrics, Tables, Figures & Evidence Types**
- **Metrics:** IAP revenue, $3B revenue milestones, DAUs, CPI, user growth figures, market valuation projections
- **Tables/Figures:** Tables are explicitly referenced in the safety subsection, though specific content is truncated in the source
- **Evidence Types:** Industry reporting, historical moderation evolution timelines, revenue/timeframe data, regulatory framework documentation, and platform safety case studies

**SECTION 3: Community (Pages 21–24)**
This section addresses the strategic implementation of community building in gaming and the historical-to-technological evolution of community safety frameworks. It covers engagement models, moderation workflows, regulatory compliance, and player experience management across global platforms and genres.

**Topics**
- Community building as a competitive strategy
- Social media vs. in-game community engagement
- Live service and event-driven retention models
- eSports as a discoverability and engagement driver
- Community management software ecosystems
- Historical evolution of video game community safety
- Transition from manual to AI-driven moderation
- Impact of social/mobile gaming on player toxicity
- Regulatory and platform safety frameworks

**Dates & Timeframes**
- 1990s: Shift to online gaming
- Late 1990s: Introduction of dedicated community managers
- 2000s: Rise of social/mobile gaming and BPO outsourcing
- Q1 2024: Monopoly Go! IAP revenue reporting period
- H1 2024: PUBG Mobile user growth and Krafton revenue reporting period
- July 2024: Launch of PUBG Mobile x Dragon Ball Super Z event
- 15 months: Timeframe for Monopoly Go! to reach $3B revenue
- 2025: Industry report publication year
- 2030: Projected valuation year for the global eSports market

**Definitions & Concepts**
- Community touchpoints, live service strategy, Partner Events, cost per installs (CPI), in-app purchases (IAP), daily active users (DAUs), battle pass, IP crossover modes, eSports discoverability, community safety, Player Experience Teams (trust and safety gatekeepers), automated moderation, multimodal input detection, Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) in gaming contexts

**Procedures & Strategies**
- Transitioning from transactional sales to immersive community curation
- Leveraging social media for player recommendations and discussion
- Implementing in-game community features and engagement loops
- Stepwise moderation evolution: player-led → admin-monitored → dedicated managers → in-house teams → outsourced BPO → AI/ML automation → real-time multimodal systems
- Player Experience Team workflows: complaint handling, guideline enforcement, AI-driven pattern recognition, proactive flagging, prosocial behavior incentivization

**Claims**
- Increased game scale and social integration correlate with higher toxicity, cheating, and fraud
- AI/ML is essential for proactive, real-time moderation across in-game and off-platform communities
- Regulatory compliance and platform-specific standards are critical for harm mitigation

**Metrics, Tables, Figures & Evidence Types**
- **Metrics:** IAP revenue, $3B revenue milestones, DAUs, CPI, user growth figures, market valuation projections
- **Tables/Figures:** Tables are explicitly referenced in the safety subsection, though specific content is truncated in the source
- **Evidence Types:** Industry reporting, historical moderation evolution timelines, revenue/timeframe data, regulatory framework documentation, and platform safety case studies
